name: Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) at UC San Diego
description: Largest computing student org at UCSD. Runs project teams, technical workshops, and a hack night cadence. Project teams ship real software with peer code review, which is the single fastest way to get your first git collaboration story.

name: Triton Software Engineering (TSE)
description: Student-run pro-bono software consultancy: small teams build real apps for nonprofit clients across a 6–9 month engagement. Application-only, looks like a real internship on a resume because it is one.

name: AI Student Collective at UC San Diego
description: Reading group + applied projects on modern ML and LLMs. Good entry point if you want a Data Scientist / ML Intern story but can't yet take graduate ML coursework.

name: Blockchain at UC San Diego
description: Student org running technical workshops on Solidity, ZK proofs, and crypto-adjacent engineering. Doubles as a recruiting funnel for crypto/fintech internships.

name: Design Co at UC San Diego
description: Product design club: portfolio reviews, design challenges, and partnerships with student-run product teams. The most direct path to a Product Design Intern portfolio at UCSD.

name: Triton AI / Robotics Project Teams
description: Hardware + software project teams (autonomous vehicles, robotics) under TESC. Hands-on embedded and applied ML work, plus a competition cadence that creates resume bullets with measurable outcomes.

name: Triton Engineering Student Council (TESC)
description: Umbrella council for ~30 engineering project teams and orgs. Best 'I don't know which engineering club to join' starting point because TESC's events introduce all of them at once.

name: Undergraduate Research Hub (URH) Programs
description: Central directory of UCSD undergrad research programs (Faculty Mentor Program, Summer Research Program, Triton Research & Experiential Learning Scholars, etc.). Most are open to first- and second-years and pair you with a faculty mentor.

name: Academic Internship Program (AIP)
description: For-credit internship program through the university. Helps you find an internship and wraps it in a graded course, which is friendlier than the open market for first-years with no prior experience.
skillsItBuilds: any role-specific skill from the placement

name: The Basement (UC San Diego startup incubator)
description: Undergrad-focused incubator on campus. Office hours with EIRs, pitch competitions, and accelerator cohorts. Great for PM and design archetypes because everything is end-to-end product work.

name: Beta Alpha Psi at UC San Diego
description: Honors org for accounting, finance, and information systems. Recruiting events with Big Four and Bay Area finance firms, plus technical workshops on Excel modeling and case prep.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi (Professional Business Fraternity)
description: Co-ed professional business fraternity at UCSD. Rush-based; once in, you get an alumni network spanning consulting, finance, marketing, and tech. Strongest signal for Consulting/Finance archetypes.

name: ALPFA at UC San Diego
description: Association of Latino Professionals For America student chapter. Mentorship, conference funding, and a corporate partner pipeline (consulting, finance, tech).

name: Ascend at UC San Diego
description: Pan-Asian business student org with strong corporate ties (consulting, finance, tech). Workshop series + national conference each year.

name: American Statistical Association at UC San Diego
description: Data and stats student org. Runs a DataFest team and applied data analysis projects. Lower-bar entry to building a Data Analyst Intern portfolio.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society (BMES) at UC San Diego
description: Bioengineering project teams, industry mentor program, and lab tours. Best entry point for Bio Research / Bioengineering Intern paths.

name: UC San Diego Career Center
description: Resume reviews, mock interviews, and recruiter-led workshops every quarter. The mock interview slots are the most underused leverage on campus for first-years.

name: IDEA Engineering Student Center
description: Jacobs School student center. Tutoring, mentor matching, scholarship programs, and a project-based makerspace component. Especially strong for first- and second-year engineering students.
